title: [Subagent] Site Auditor
prompt:
[SUBAGENT: Site Auditor]

TASK: You are the Site Auditor for the Xpose Website Rebuild team. Conduct a comprehensive audit of the Xpose Market website.

OBJECTIVES:
1. Find and inspect the LIVE Xpose Market website. Get the URL (likely xposemarket.com or similar). Take full screenshots of the homepage and key pages. Document:
   - Current messaging and value proposition
   - CTA button prominence and placement
   - Social proof elements (testimonials, case studies, client logos)
   - UX issues, broken elements, unclear copy
   - Mobile responsiveness
   - Form conversion funnels (if available)

2. Inspect the LOCAL xposemarket-site/ codebase in the workspace (if it exists as a submodule or folder). Review:
   - Key page templates and copy
   - Current features and components
   - Any known issues or TODOs in the code

3. Research 2-3 COMPETITOR AGENCY WEBSITES offering similar services (digital marketing, SEO, brand building, web design for local businesses). For each:
   - Company name and URL
   - How their messaging differs from Xpose
   - What they do better (CTA clarity, social proof, mobile UX, etc.)
   - Screenshot examples

4. COMPARE and identify TOP 3-5 CONVERSION BLOCKERS/OPPORTUNITIES:
   - What's missing or unclear on Xpose that competitors have
   - What Xpose does better
   - Specific fixes that would have HIGH impact on conversions

DELIVERABLE:
Return a structured audit report with:
- **Current State**: Xpose website URL, current messaging, UX snapshot, conversion blockers
- **Competitors**: 2-3 agency findings (names, what they do better)
- **Top 3-5 Improvements**: For each:
  - Problem statement
  - Recommended fix
  - Conversion impact (high/medium/low)
  - Visual example or before/after if possible
- **Prioritization rationale**

Be specific and thorough — this kicksoff the weekly rebuild cycle.


CONSTRAINTS:
• Do not make changes to the site; report findings only
• Use browser automation to inspect and screenshot current state
• Focus on conversion signals and trust elements, not cosmetics
• Provide specific evidence: link to exact pages, cite exact copy issues, include competitor comparison URLs
• Weekly cadence: deliver audit by end of week

SUCCESS CRITERIA: Weekly audit complete with 3–5 prioritized recommendations, screenshot evidence, before/after comparisons, and concrete next steps for the builder.

plan.description: Return extracted data to parent task
